dashboard.noProjectsPrompt: No research projects yet. Start with a demo or create your first.
dashboard.opportunitiesTitle: New research angles I found for you
dashboard.activeTopicsSubtitle: Pick up where you left off.
dashboard.trendsSubtitle: What's heating up, cooling down, and still wide open. Tap any row for the evidence.
dashboard.noTrends: No trends data yet. Generate clusters from the current paper corpus (or seed data if the library is empty).

welcome.title: Welcome to Atlas
welcome.subtitle: Your AI research partner, from first search to final draft.

gather.title: Find what matters
gather.body: Discover and rank the papers that actually move your thinking, not just what's cited most.

analyze.title: See the gaps
analyze.body: Cross-check hundreds of claims, surface contradictions, and spot open problems worth your time.

write.title: Write with sources
write.body: Every sentence links back to evidence. Share clean drafts with your advisor in minutes.

cta.configureModel: I've got an API key — let's set it up

modelStatus.demoModeHint: You're using a local demo engine. Connect a model to run real research.
modelStatus.noneHint: Connect at least one model to run paid operations.

tokens.budgetWarnNearCap: You're at {percent}% of today's cap. Switch to Economy mode?

budgets.title: Budgets
budgets.subtitle: Where your tokens went — by model and by research stage.
budgets.byModelSubtitle: Each model's share of this month's tokens.
budgets.byStageSubtitle: Which of the 6 pipeline stages consumed which tokens.
budgets.trendSubtitle: Token usage per day this month.
budgets.noUsage: No usage recorded this month yet.

filters.noMatches: No activity matches the current filter.

topics.title: No research projects yet
topics.body: A topic is one research project — its papers, claims, drafts, and decisions all in one place.

papers.title: No papers in this topic
papers.body: Start by searching with a few keywords or adding an arXiv ID you already have in mind.

gaps.title: No gaps detected yet
gaps.body: Once you have 15+ papers, I can scan for genuine open problems in this area.

contradictions.title: No contradictions on record
contradictions.body: This means either your literature agrees or nobody's checked yet. Usually I find 2–5 in a typical pool.

drafts.title: No draft yet
drafts.body: I can draft any section from your papers. Want to start with the Introduction?

reports.title: No reports generated
reports.body: Reports are sharable PDFs for your advisor — you pick which sections to include.

reports.title: Advisor Reports
reports.subtitle: Generate clean PDFs tailored to what your advisor wants to see.
reports.noMatches: No reports match the current filter.
reports.customPlaceholder: e.g. Focus on the contrast with the GPT-4 baseline. Tone: concise, no hedging. Audience: my advisor.
reports.customHint: Forwarded into every section draft as additional author guidance. Regenerates sections instead of reusing older drafts.
reports.someBlocked: Some selections need a later stage — they'll be skipped on generate
reports.pickAtLeastOne: Pick at least one template above.

abstract_only.name: Abstract check
abstract_only.description: Just the abstract. Perfect for a quick 5-minute review.

abstract_intro.name: Pitch package
abstract_intro.description: Abstract + Introduction. Lets your advisor say yes/no to the angle.

deep_pitch.name: Deep pitch
deep_pitch.description: Abstract + Introduction + Related Work + Method outline.

full_review.name: Full review
full_review.description: Complete draft with self-assessment and risk list.

reports.confidenceNote: AI-generated paragraphs carry a confidence tag so your advisor sees what's solid vs what needs review.
reports.emptyLibrary: Reports you generate will appear here.
reports.blockedHint: This template needs {stage} stage to be reached. You're currently at {current}.

celebration.firstGap: 💡 Found {count} research gaps worth thinking about

nextStage.allDoneBody: The pipeline is complete. Review artifacts, draft your paper, or start a new topic.

research.title: Research
research.subtitle: Your research areas (domains) and the topics inside them.
research.searchPlaceholder: Search topic name, description, or domain...
research.emptyBody: Start a topic to begin literature discovery, claim extraction, and drafting.

reconcile.title: Reconcile unassigned topics
reconcile.subtitle: Smart suggestions based on topic name and description overlap with your existing domains. Nothing is applied until you confirm — one click per row, or apply-all once you've reviewed.
reconcile.allCleanBody: Every topic has a domain — nothing to reconcile.
reconcile.lowConfidence: Low match — consider creating a new domain

library.title: Paper library
library.emptyBody: Ingest a paper from arXiv or import a BibTeX file to get started.

discover.heroBody: Trending clusters, open problems worth your time, and honest direction recommendations — built from your paper pool and the broader literature.
discover.emptyBody: I need a paper pool with some history to surface what the field is doing. Add a topic, ingest papers, then come back here.
discover.hotSubtitle: Clusters with the biggest year-over-year jumps in the literature
discover.openProblemsSubtitle: High-score clusters that the field hasn't flooded yet
discover.directionsSubtitle: Three I'd consider — with both upside and honest risks
discover.coolingDetail: Down {pct}% YoY — field may be shifting attention elsewhere. Not always bad (mature methods stabilize), but factor it in.
discover.honestWarning: Honest warning: +{pct}% YoY is crowded territory. Differentiation will be harder here than headline numbers suggest.
discover.whyMatters: High publishability score with moderate competition — a good window for differentiation.
discover.recSummaryFallback: Publishability {score}/10 with {velocity}% YoY momentum — strong venue presence and room to differentiate.

agents.title: Models
agents.subtitle: Manage the LLMs that do the work — generators, judges, challengers.
agents.providersSubtitle: LLM providers detected from your environment variables.
agents.providersEmpty: No providers detected. Set API key environment variables (e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) to enable providers.
agents.tierRoutingSubtitle: How tasks are routed to models by complexity tier.
agents.tierLightDesc: Fast, cheap tasks — summaries, formatting, classification
agents.tierMediumDesc: Balanced tasks — drafting, analysis, claim extraction
agents.tierHeavyDesc: Complex tasks — deep reasoning, adversarial review, gap detection
agents.registeredSubtitle: User-registered LLM agents for generation, judging, and challenging.
agents.noAgentsHint: Complete onboarding or register one manually.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Preferences, scoring rules, and connected accounts.

scoring.title: Scoring rules
scoring.description: Set how strictly each research stage is graded, and choose whether the system can roll back artifacts that fail the bar.
scoring.pageDescription: Each topic goes through six stages — Init, Build, Analyze, Propose, Experiment, Write — and each one gets graded by an LLM judge. The numbers below are the per-stage pass marks. You can adjust how strict they are, and whether the system is allowed to roll back work that falls short.
scoring.failGateTitle: When the system fails a gate
scoring.failGateDescription: Shadow just records the verdict — your work always passes through. Live actually rolls the stage back when an artifact misses the bar. Stay on Shadow until you’ve seen at least two weeks of grading on real topics.
scoring.liveWarning: Live mode is on. Artifacts below the pass mark will be auto-rolled-back the next time a stage runs. Re-tune the pass marks on real artifacts before relying on this.
scoring.strictnessDescription: Pick a tier per topic on the topic detail page. Each tier targets a different publication bar.
scoring.tierDescEconomy: Lightest review. The system passes most artifacts; only the worst are flagged.
scoring.tierDescStandard: Balanced. Each stage is graded against the typical bar for a B-tier paper. Recommended.
scoring.tierDescPremium: Strict. Two judges score each gate; threshold targets A-tier venues.
scoring.advancedDescription: The actual numeric thresholds. Recompute them from the bundled anchor papers if you want to recalibrate.

trends.title: Trend pipeline
trends.description: Regenerate the research-trend clusters that drive the dashboard carousel and the trends explorer.

agentsKeys.title: Agents & API keys
agentsKeys.description: Add, remove, and pair the LLM agents the system uses for generation, judging, and adversarial review.

providers.title: Provider configuration
providers.description: Manage LLM providers, auto-detect API keys, and configure tier routing.
providers.noProviders: No providers detected. Set an API key env var (e.g. DEEPSEEK_API_KEY) to enable a provider.
providers.tierRoutingSubtitle: How tasks are routed to providers by complexity tier.

experiments.title: Experiments
experiments.empty: No experiments yet — click New to start an autonomous loop.

roadmap.graphHint: Click a stage node to inspect every primitive, decision, and invariant that ran there.

drawer.description: Inspect every primitive, decision, and invariant for this stage.
drawer.emptyTrace: No activity recorded for this stage yet.
drawer.emptyArtifacts: No artifacts recorded for this stage yet.
